explanation: The question asks about the correct statement regarding the Indian Constitution. Statement 1 is correct because the Preamble, which is part of the Constitution, declares India to be a Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic. Statement 2 is incorrect as the Preamble was amended by the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976, to include the words 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity'.

This question pertains to the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs) in the Indian Constitution. Statement 1 is correct as the DPSPs aim to establish a welfare state by promoting social and economic democracy. Statement 2 is incorrect because DPSPs are not justiciable, meaning they cannot be enforced by any court, unlike Fundamental Rights.

The question asks which of the listed countries share a land border with Ukraine. Ukraine shares land borders with Poland and Slovakia. Therefore, only two of the mentioned countries share a land border with Ukraine.

The question is about the correct statement regarding the Indian Parliament. Statement 1 is correct as the Lok Sabha is the lower house of the Parliament, and its members are directly elected by the people. Statement 2 is incorrect because the Rajya Sabha is the upper house, and its members are indirectly elected by the elected members of the State Legislative Assemblies.

This question concerns the powers of the President of India. Statement 1 is correct as the President can grant pardons, reprieves, respites, or remissions of punishment, or suspend, remit, or commute the sentence of any person convicted of an offense. Statement 2 is incorrect because the President acts on the advice of the Council of Ministers, not independently, in most matters.

The question asks about the correct statement regarding the Supreme Court of India. Statement 1 is correct as the Supreme Court has the power of judicial review, meaning it can review any law or executive order to determine its constitutionality. Statement 2 is incorrect because the Supreme Court's jurisdiction is not limited to advisory opinions; it also hears appeals and original cases.

This question is about the Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution. Statement 1 is correct as the Right to Equality (Articles 14-18) ensures equal treatment before the law and prohibits discrimination. Statement 2 is correct as the Right to Freedom (Articles 19-22) includes freedom of speech and expression, assembly, association, movement, residence, and profession. Statement 3 is correct as the Right against Exploitation (Articles 23-24) prohibits forced labor and child labor.

The question asks about the correct statement regarding the Election Commission of India. Statement I is correct as the Election Commission is an independent constitutional body responsible for conducting elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, the office of the President, and the office of the Vice-President. Statement II is incorrect because the Election Commission does not have the power to dismiss government officials; that power lies with the executive.

This question concerns the Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India. Statement I is correct as the GST Council is a constitutional body that makes recommendations on GST rates, exemptions, and other related matters. Statement II is incorrect because while GST aims to simplify indirect taxation, it does not necessarily mean a uniform rate of 18% for all goods and services; there are multiple tax slabs.

The question asks about the correct statement regarding the Panchayati Raj system in India. Statement 1 is correct as the Panchayati Raj system is a form of local self-government at the village level. Statement 2 is correct as the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions. Statement 3 is incorrect because the Panchayati Raj system was introduced in India in 1959, not 2002.

This question is about the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) of India. Statement 1 is correct as the NHRC is a statutory body established under the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993, to protect and promote human rights. Statement 2 is incorrect because the NHRC can only make recommendations to the government; it does not have the power to punish offenders.

The question asks about the correct statement regarding the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India. Statement 1 is correct as the CAG is an independent constitutional authority responsible for auditing all receipts and expenditures of the Union and State governments. Statement 2 is correct as the CAG audits government companies and corporations. Statement 3 is correct as the CAG submits its audit reports to the President, who then causes them to be laid before each House of Parliament. Statement 4 is incorrect as the CAG is appointed by the President, not elected.

This question is about the Attorney General of India. Statement 2 is correct as the Attorney General is the highest law officer in the country and advises the Government of India on legal matters. Statement 3 is correct as the Attorney General has the right to speak and to take part in the proceedings of both Houses of Parliament, but without a vote. Statement 4 is incorrect as the Attorney General is appointed by the President, not elected.

The question asks about the correct statement regarding the Vice-President of India. Statement 3 is correct as the Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha. Statement 4 is incorrect as the Vice-President is elected by an electoral college consisting of all the members of both Houses of Parliament, not just the Lok Sabha.

This question is about the Council of Ministers in India. Statement 4 is correct as the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha, meaning they must enjoy the confidence of the majority in the Lok Sabha.
